name: dbt MCP Server
what_it_does: Official MCP server from dbt Labs that bridges AI agents with dbt projects. Provides tools for SQL execution, natural language to SQL conversion, semantic layer querying, project discovery (models, macros, sources, lineage), CLI operations (build, test, compile), dbt Cloud admin API access, and YAML/SQL code generation for dbt models.
use_cases: Natural language querying of dbt-managed data warehouses
Exploring dbt project structure, lineage, and health from AI tools
Generating dbt model YAML and staging SQL via AI
Running dbt builds, tests, and compilation through AI agents
Semantic layer metric querying and discovery
Cross-project resource search in dbt Platform environments
not_for: General database access without dbt (use dbhub or db-mcp-server instead)
Production job orchestration (use dbt Cloud scheduler for that)
best_when: You have an existing dbt project (Core or Cloud) and want AI agents to explore, query, build, and generate code within the dbt ecosystem.
avoid_when: You don't use dbt, or you need general-purpose database access unrelated to dbt projects.

auth.notes: dbt Platform features require dbt Cloud credentials and API tokens. Local dbt Core features use local project config. Admin API access requires separate API keys.

pricing.notes: Apache-2.0 license. The MCP server is free but dbt Cloud features require a dbt Cloud account (which has its own pricing tiers).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Official dbt Labs project with community support channels. Error handling quality not explicitly documented for agent consumers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations (discovery, lineage, semantic queries) are idempotent. Build/execute operations are not. Job triggers via admin API are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Feature availability depends on whether using dbt Core, Fusion, or Cloud - agents may call unavailable tools
MCPB distribution format requires MCPB-aware clients for zero-config setup
text_to_sql quality depends heavily on project context and model documentation
Cross-project search is alpha-stage and may be unreliable
Admin API operations (job triggers) have real side effects in production
